docs.title: congress_py
docs.text:
congress_py is an unofficial Python SDK, CLI, and read-only MCP server for the Congress.gov API. It provides a small, testable interface for reading congressional sessions, bills, bill actions, and bill summaries.

The project is intended to stay compact and readable. The SDK remains the source of truth; the CLI and MCP tools call the SDK instead of duplicating API logic.

Unofficial project

This project is not affiliated with Congress.gov, the Library of Congress, Congress, or the U.S. government.

No advice

This project does not provide legal, legislative, lobbying, financial, compliance, or policy advice. Verify important information against official Congress.gov records.

docs.title: What is included
docs.text:
• A CongressClient SDK for read-only API access.
  • Typed models for bills, bill actions, and bill summaries.
  • A JSON-first congress CLI.
  • A stdio MCP server for selected read-only bill workflows.
  • Mocked tests for SDK, CLI, and MCP behavior.

docs.title: Start here
docs.text:
• Installation
  • Quickstart
  • CLI usage
  • SDK guide
  • MCP server
  • API reference

docs.title: API Reference
docs.text: This page is generated from the SDK modules only. CLI documentation is hand-written in CLI.

docs.title: congress_py.client
docs.text: Client for the Congress.gov API.

docs.title: CongressClient
docs.text:
Client for interacting with Congress API endpoints.

Parameters:

Name Type Description Default api_key str

API key provided by api.congress.gov.

required session

Optional requests.Session-compatible object used to make HTTP requests. If omitted, a new requests.Session is created. This is mainly useful for tests or for callers that need custom session configuration.

None

Attributes:

Name Type Description api_key

Congress.gov API key used for requests.

base_url

Base URL for the Congress.gov API.

congress_url

Base URL for congress/session endpoints.

bill_url

Base URL for bill endpoints.

session

HTTP session used to make requests.

docs.title: get_bill(congress, bill_type, number)
docs.text:
Return a bill for a given congress, bill type, and number.

Parameters:

Name Type Description Default congress int

A Congress number, such as 118.

required bill_type str

Congress.gov bill type code, such as "hr" or "s".

required number int

Bill number within that congress and bill type.

required

Returns:

Name Type Description Bill

A Bill model created from the API response's bill object.

docs.title: get_bill_actions(congress, bill_type, number)
docs.text:
Return actions for a given bill.

Parameters:

Name Type Description Default congress int

Congress number, such as 118.

required bill_type str

Congress.gov bill type code, such as "hr" or "s".

required number int

Bill number within that congress and bill type.

required

Returns:

Type Description

list[BillAction]: Actions parsed from the API response's actions list.

docs.title: get_bill_summaries(congress, bill_type, number)
docs.text:
Return summaries for a given bill.

Parameters:

Name Type Description Default congress int

Congress number, such as 118.

required bill_type str

Congress.gov bill type code, such as "hr" or "s".

required number int

Bill number within that congress and bill type.

required

Returns:

Type Description

list[BillSummary]: Summaries parsed from the API response's summaries list.

docs.title: get_bills(session=None, limit=20, offset=0)
docs.text:
Return a page of bills, optionally filtered by Congress number.

Parameters:

Name Type Description Default session

Optional Congress number used to filter results, such as 118.

None limit int

Maximum number of bills to request from the API.

20 offset int

Number of records to skip before returning results.

0

Returns:

Type Description

list[Bill]: Bills parsed from the API response's bills list.

docs.title: get_congresses()
docs.text:
Return congressional session records.

Returns:

Type Description

list[dict]: The raw congresses list from the API response.

docs.title: get_current_session()
docs.text:
Return the current congressional session.

Returns:

Type Description

A named tuple containing the fields name, endYear, and chambers.

docs.title: iter_bills(session=None, limit=20, max_pages=None)
docs.text:
Yield bills across pages until no bills are returned.

Parameters:

Name Type Description Default session

Optional Congress number used to filter results, such as 118.

None limit int

Number of bills to request per API page.

20 max_pages

Optional maximum number of pages to fetch. If omitted, pages are fetched until the API returns an empty bills list.

None

Yields:

Name Type Description Bill

Bills parsed from each API response's bills list.

docs.title: list_recent_bills(limit=10)
docs.text:
Return a conservative first page of recent bills.

Parameters:

Name Type Description Default limit int

Maximum number of bills to request. Must be between 1 and 250.

10

Returns:

Type Description

list[Bill]: Bills parsed from the API response's bills list.

Raises:

Type Description ValueError

If limit is outside the supported range.

docs.title: congress_py.models
docs.text: Data models for Congress API responses.

docs.title: Bill dataclass
docs.text:
Bill data returned by Congress.gov.

Attributes:

Name Type Description congress int

Congress number, such as 118.

latest_action_date str

Date of the latest recorded action.

latest_action_text str

Description of the latest recorded action.

number str

Bill number within its bill type.

origin_chamber str

Chamber where the bill originated.

title str

Official bill title.

bill_type str

Congress.gov bill type code, such as "hr" or "s".

update_date str

Date the bill record was last updated.

update_including_text bool

Whether the bill text was included in the latest update.

url Optional[str]

Optional Congress.gov API URL for the bill record.

docs.title: from_api_dict(bill_data) classmethod
docs.text: Create a Bill from an API response dictionary.

docs.title: BillAction dataclass
docs.text:
Legislative action recorded for a bill.

Attributes:

Name Type Description action_date Optional[str]

Date the action occurred, if provided by the API.

text Optional[str]

Description of the action, if provided by the API.

action_type Optional[str]

Congress.gov action type value, if provided.

source_system Optional[Dict[str, Any]]

Source system metadata from Congress.gov, if provided.

url Optional[str]

Optional Congress.gov API URL for the action record.

docs.title: from_api_dict(action_data) classmethod
docs.text: Create a BillAction from an API response dictionary.

docs.title: BillSummary dataclass
docs.text:
Summary text available for a bill.

Attributes:

Name Type Description action_date Optional[str]

Date associated with the summary action, if provided.

text Optional[str]

Summary text, if provided by the API.

update_date Optional[str]

Date the summary record was last updated, if provided.

version_code Optional[str]

Congress.gov summary version code, if provided.

action_desc Optional[str]

Description of the action associated with the summary, if provided.

docs.title: from_api_dict(summary_data) classmethod
docs.text: Create a BillSummary from an API response dictionary.

Bases: Exception

Base exception for congress_py errors.

Bases: CongressAPIError

Base exception for authentication-related errors.

docs.title: InvalidAPIKeyError
docs.text:
Bases: CongressAuthError

Raised when Congress.gov rejects the provided API key.

docs.title: MissingAPIKeyError
docs.text:
Bases: CongressAuthError

Raised when no Congress.gov API key is available.

docs.title: Changelog
docs.text: All notable user-facing changes to this project will be documented in this file. This changelog follows the Keep a Changelog style and uses the package version from pyproject.toml.

docs.title: [Unreleased]
docs.text: • No unreleased changes.

docs.title: Added
docs.text:
• MkDocs documentation site using MkDocs Material.
  • Documentation pages for installation, quickstart, CLI usage, SDK usage, API reference, contributing, and changelog.
  • mkdocstrings-generated API reference for client.py, models.py, and exceptions.py.
  • CongressClient.list_recent_bills(limit=10) convenience method for safe recent bill discovery.
  • Optional MCP server support with read-only bill tools.
  • congress mcp-start for local stdio MCP server testing.
  • Dockerfile for running the MCP server with CONGRESS_API_KEY supplied at runtime.

docs.title: Changed
docs.text: • Shortened the README into a front door for the project and documentation site.

docs.title: Added
docs.text:
• Initial congress_py SDK package using a src layout.
  • CongressClient with Congress session and bill endpoints.
  • Typed models for bills, bill actions, and bill summaries.
  • Typer-based congress CLI.
  • CLI credential resolution using --api-key, CONGRESS_API_KEY, and ~/.congress/config.toml.
  • Bill workflow commands for details, actions, and summaries.
  • Bill listing pagination and multi-page iteration.
  • Mocked SDK and CLI tests.

docs.title: Changed
docs.text:
• Refactored the project into the congress_py package.
  • Updated README examples as SDK and CLI behavior expanded.

docs.title: Fixed
docs.text:
• Supported the /congress/current item response shape.
  • Made bill URL optional for single-bill API responses.
  • Fixed CLI JSON serialization for dataclass and namedtuple outputs.

docs.title: CLI
docs.text: The package installs a congress command. The CLI is intentionally thin: it resolves configuration, calls CongressClient, and prints JSON.

docs.title: Credentials
docs.text:
Credential resolution order:

  1. Explicit --api-key
  2. CONGRESS_API_KEY
  3. ~/.congress/config.toml

Only congress configure prompts for an API key. Other commands fail clearly when no key is available.

congress configure

The config file format is:

[auth]
api_key = "your-api-key-here"

Do not commit this file or print the full API key in logs.

docs.title: Congress commands
docs.text:
congress congress current
congress congress list

docs.title: Bill commands
docs.text:
congress bills list
congress bills list --session 118
congress bills list --limit 50
congress bills list --session 118 --limit 25 --offset 100
congress bills list --session 118 --limit 25 --pages 3
congress bills get 118 hr 7437
congress bills actions 118 hr 7437
congress bills summaries 118 hr 7437

--limit controls the number of bills requested per API call. --offset controls the starting offset in single-page mode. --pages switches to multi-page iteration through the SDK iterator. When --pages is provided, --offset is ignored because the current iterator starts from the first page.

docs.title: One-command API key
docs.text:
congress --api-key your_api_key_here bills get 118 hr 7437

Prefer environment variables or local CLI config for routine use so secrets do not appear in shell history.

docs.title: MCP server
docs.text:
congress mcp-start

mcp-start starts the read-only stdio MCP server. It uses the same credential resolution order as the rest of the CLI and is intended for local MCP client testing.

docs.title: Contributing
docs.text: This is an unofficial portfolio project. Contributions should keep the project small, readable, testable, and safe to publish.

docs.title: Development setup
docs.text:
git clone https://github.com/talaniz/congress.py.git
cd congress.py
python3 -m venv .venv
.venv/bin/python -m pip install -e ".[dev]"

For documentation work:

.venv/bin/python -m pip install -e ".[docs]"

docs.title: Tests
docs.text:
.venv/bin/python -m pytest

Default tests should use mocked HTTP. Avoid live Congress.gov calls in normal test runs.

docs.title: Documentation
docs.text:
Build docs before submitting documentation changes:

.venv/bin/python -m mkdocs build --strict

Use hand-written docs for CLI behavior. Use the API reference page for mkdocstrings-generated SDK documentation from client.py, models.py, and exceptions.py.

docs.title: Scope
docs.text:
• Keep SDK methods thin and predictable.
  • Keep CLI commands thin and JSON-first.
  • Do not duplicate API request logic outside CongressClient.
  • Do not add persistence, caching, authentication systems, or advice-oriented features unless explicitly approved.
  • Do not imply affiliation with Congress.gov, the Library of Congress, Congress, or the U.S. government.

docs.title: Security
docs.text: Do not commit API keys, .env files, local config files, or private notes. Examples must not contain secrets. Error messages and docs should never expose full API keys.

docs.title: Requirements
docs.text:
• Python 3.9 or newer
  • A Congress.gov API key

Request an API key from the official Congress.gov API site. Keep the key local and do not commit it to source control.

docs.title: Install from PyPI
docs.text: python -m pip install congress-py

docs.title: Install MCP support from PyPI
docs.text: python -m pip install "congress-py[mcp]"

docs.title: Install from source
docs.text:
git clone https://github.com/talaniz/congress.py.git
cd congress.py
python3 -m venv .venv
.venv/bin/python -m pip install -e .

docs.title: Install development tools
docs.text: .venv/bin/python -m pip install -e ".[dev]"

docs.title: Install documentation tools
docs.text: .venv/bin/python -m pip install -e ".[docs]"

docs.title: Install MCP support from source
docs.text: .venv/bin/python -m pip install -e ".[mcp]"

docs.title: Build the docs locally
docs.text:
.venv/bin/python -m mkdocs build --strict

To preview the docs locally:

.venv/bin/python -m mkdocs serve

docs.title: MCP Server
docs.text: congress_py includes an optional read-only MCP server for selected Congress.gov bill workflows. The MCP layer calls CongressClient; it does not duplicate Congress.gov HTTP logic.

docs.title: Install MCP Support
docs.text:
From PyPI:

python -m pip install "congress-py[mcp]"

For local development:

.venv/bin/python -m pip install -e ".[mcp]"

docs.title: Available Tools
docs.text:
• get_bill
  • get_bill_actions
  • get_bill_summaries
  • list_recent_bills

Tool output uses the same snake_case JSON-compatible shape as the SDK and CLI.

docs.title: Start Locally
docs.text:
The easiest local entry point is:

congress mcp-start

The command resolves credentials in the same order as the rest of the CLI:

  1. --api-key
  2. CONGRESS_API_KEY
  3. ~/.congress/config.toml

You can also start the MCP module directly:

CONGRESS_API_KEY=your_api_key_here python -m congress_py.mcp_server

The server uses stdio transport for local MCP clients.

docs.title: Docker
docs.text:
Build the image locally:

docker build -t congress-py-mcp .

Run it with the API key supplied at runtime:

docker run --rm -i -e CONGRESS_API_KEY=your_api_key_here congress-py-mcp

Do not pass API keys as Docker build arguments. Runtime environment variables avoid baking secrets into the image.

The container entry point runs the Python module directly:

ENTRYPOINT ["python", "-m", "congress_py.mcp_server"]

docs.title: Example Client Configuration
docs.text:
For an MCP client that launches a local command, configure the command to run:

congress mcp-start

For Docker-based local testing, configure the client to run the docker run command above with CONGRESS_API_KEY supplied by the environment.

docs.title: Configure an API key
docs.text:
The SDK accepts an explicit API key. It does not read local CLI config files.

export CONGRESS_API_KEY=your_api_key_here

For CLI-only convenience, you can save a local key:

congress configure

The CLI resolves credentials in this order:

  1. --api-key
  2. CONGRESS_API_KEY
  3. ~/.congress/config.toml

Normal CLI commands do not prompt for credentials.

docs.title: Use the SDK
docs.text:
import os

from congress_py import CongressClient

client = CongressClient(api_key=os.environ["CONGRESS_API_KEY"])

current = client.get_current_session()
print(current.name)

bills = client.get_bills(session=118, limit=20, offset=0)
print(bills[0].title)

bill = client.get_bill(118, "hr", 7437)
actions = client.get_bill_actions(118, "hr", 7437)
summaries = client.get_bill_summaries(118, "hr", 7437)

print(bill.title)
print(actions[0].text)
print(summaries[0].text)

docs.title: Use the CLI
docs.text:
congress congress current
congress bills list --session 118 --limit 20
congress bills get 118 hr 7437
congress bills actions 118 hr 7437
congress bills summaries 118 hr 7437

CLI output is JSON by default.

docs.title: Start the MCP server
docs.text:
Install the MCP extra, then start the local stdio server:

.venv/bin/python -m pip install -e ".[mcp]"
congress mcp-start

docs.title: SDK
docs.text: The SDK centers on CongressClient. It owns endpoint construction, API-key handling, query parameters, HTTP session usage, timeouts, status handling, JSON decoding, pagination helpers, and conversion into typed models.

docs.title: Create a client
docs.text:
import os

from congress_py import CongressClient

client = CongressClient(api_key=os.environ["CONGRESS_API_KEY"])

The SDK may be passed a custom requests.Session-compatible object, which is mainly useful for tests or callers that need custom session configuration.

docs.title: Congressional sessions
docs.text:
current = client.get_current_session()
congresses = client.get_congresses()

get_current_session() returns a named tuple with name, endYear, and chambers. get_congresses() returns the raw congresses list from the API.

docs.title: Bills
docs.text:
bills = client.get_bills(session=118, limit=20, offset=0)
recent_bills = client.list_recent_bills(limit=10)
bill = client.get_bill(118, "hr", 7437)

get_bills() returns a single page of Bill models. It intentionally returns only list[Bill]; raw pagination metadata is not exposed by this method. list_recent_bills() is a conservative convenience wrapper for the first bill listing page and accepts limits from 1 through 250.

docs.title: Iterating bill pages
docs.text:
for bill in client.iter_bills(session=118, limit=20, max_pages=3):
    print(bill.number, bill.title)

iter_bills() fetches pages with increasing offsets and stops when the API returns an empty page or when max_pages is reached.

docs.title: Bill workflows
docs.text:
bill = client.get_bill(118, "hr", 7437)
actions = client.get_bill_actions(118, "hr", 7437)
summaries = client.get_bill_summaries(118, "hr", 7437)

These methods return Bill, list[BillAction], and list[BillSummary].

docs.title: Error handling
docs.text: Project-specific authentication exceptions live in congress_py.exceptions. MissingAPIKeyError is used when no key is available from the expected source. InvalidAPIKeyError is reserved for cases where Congress.gov rejects a provided key.
